name: China Foods Holdings Ltd.
business: China Foods Holdings Ltd. develops, markets, promotes, and distributes customized healthcare products including supplements, healthy snacks, meal replacements, and nutritional consultation services, primarily in China and Hong Kong. It also sells wine products sourced from France. The company operates through its wholly owned subsidiary Guangzhou Xiao Xiang Health Industry Company Limited.
namedCustomers: Hunan Wuyouzhongle Culture Media Co., Ltd.
Hunan Lezizi Culture Media Co., Ltd.
Jiangxi Tongjie New Materials Co., Ltd.
Guangdong Chuanrun Film and Television Communication Co., Ltd.
namedSuppliers: Shaanxi Bioshengde Biomedical Technology Co., Ltd
Guangzhou Beidi Packaging and Printing Co., Ltd.

exposures.sentence: Our health products are designed to help enhance immunity and improve general wellbeing.
We anticipate collaborating with established e-commerce platforms and key social media channels, including WeChat, TikTok, and Xiaohongshu.

Having launched our dedicated nutrition consulting service via a prominent social media platform in late 2020, we are now able to seamlessly offer pre-purchase advice and comprehensive after-sales support.

tamClaims.claim: The broader health and wellness market is projected to reach approximately RMB 11.4 trillion in 2026.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Frost & Sullivan and the China Business Industry Research Institute, the broader health and wellness market is projected to reach approximately RMB 11.4 trillion in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: The functional foods and health supplements sector is expected to expand to RMB 483.3 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: Within this ecosystem, the functional foods and health supplements sector is expected to expand to RMB 483.3 billion, driven heavily by younger demographics adopting preventive health routines.

tamClaims.claim: Physical healthcare products market historically tracking toward RMB 4.2 trillion.
tamClaims.sentence: Simultaneously, the medical products segment is rapidly evolving from basic consumables, such as face masks, into smart health management appliances, supporting a physical healthcare products market historically tracking toward RMB 4.2 trillion.
